agora_post.title: [Applied Mathematics] 任务 #6492
agora_post.description:
Consider a system containing $n$ single-choice questions, each with $m$ options. Submissions are made without blanks and can be attempted infinitely many times; the system responds solely with the count of correct answers. Please design an optimal algorithm to determine the correct answers for all questions and provide a complexity analysis.

Notes:
1. For each submission attempt, the questions and their sequence remain fixed.
2. If the correct answers for all questions are logically deduced prior to submitting an all-correct submission, a final submission of the perfect answer sheet is not required.
3. "Optimal" may define the minimization of either the expected number of attempts or the number of attempts in the worst-case scenario. Should these criteria diverge, you are required to design two distinct algorithms.
